6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietorisCore
7	
8	/-!
9	# Mayer-Vietoris on a 2-affine cover and Čech-acyclic cover infrastructure
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 2) contains the 2-affine cover
12	Mayer-Vietoris theorems, the cover-totality bridges (universe-shift
13	`LinearEquiv`s, `finrank` / `Module.Finite` transports), the
14	`IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers, the comparison-iso typeclass
15	`HasCechToHModuleIso`, and the existence-bundled
16	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` predicate together with the producer that
17	fires `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` from a `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover`
18	hypothesis.
19	
20	This is cohort 2 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
21	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
22	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean` (this file): 2-affine cover MV +
23	  cover-totality bridges + `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers +
24	  `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
25	
26	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
27	-/
28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u v w w'
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	section AffineCoverMVSquare
38	
39	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): a bundled 2-affine cover of a scheme
40	together with the affineness of the pairwise intersection. The geometric input
41	to the abstract Mayer-Vietoris LES `HModule'_sequence_exact` consumes — packaged
42	to record the affineness conditions and the cover-totality hypothesis that the
43	abstract MV-square does not capture. The accessor `toMayerVietorisSquare`
44	produces the underlying Mathlib `MayerVietorisSquare`.
45	
46	Mathematically: a 2-affine open cover of `X` whose pairwise intersection is also
47	affine. The cover hypothesis `cover : U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤` is load-bearing for downstream
48	Serre-finiteness use (it identifies the `MayerVietorisSquare` corner `X₄` with
49	the whole scheme `X`). -/
50	structure AffineCoverMVSquare (X : Scheme.{u}) where
51	  /-- First affine open of the cover. -/
52	  U₁ : X.Opens
53	  /-- Second affine open of the cover. -/
54	  U₂ : X.Opens
55	  /-- `U₁` is affine. -/
56	  isAffineOpen_U₁ : IsAffineOpen U₁
57	  /-- `U₂` is affine. -/
58	  isAffineOpen_U₂ : IsAffineOpen U₂
59	  /-- The intersection `U₁ ⊓ U₂` is affine. -/
60	  isAffineOpen_inf : IsAffineOpen (U₁ ⊓ U₂)
61	  /-- Total cover: `U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤`. -/
62	  cover : U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤
63	
64	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): the underlying abstract Mayer-Vietoris

155	/-- Iter-031 cover-totality source-object identification. For a category `C`
156	with terminal object `T`, the sheafification of the free `k`-module on the
157	representable presheaf at `T` is canonically isomorphic to the constant sheaf
158	at `ModuleCat.of k k`. This is the natural-iso prerequisite for identifying
159	`HModule' k F n T` (open-evaluation cohomology at the terminal `T`, iter-014)
160	with `HModule k F n` (global cohomology, iter-009): both are `Ext` groups
161	whose source objects are made canonically isomorphic by this iso.
162	
163	Assembled from three Mathlib pieces, mirroring the structure
164	`constantSheaf J D = Functor.const Cᵒᵖ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J D`:
165	1. `yoneda.obj T ≅ (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj PUnit` — the terminal-collapse
166	   of the representable, by `IsTerminal.from`/`IsTerminal.hom_ext`.
167	2. `Functor.constComp _ PUnit (ModuleCat.free k)` — the identity
168	   `(Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj PUnit ⋙ ModuleCat.free k =
169	    (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj ((ModuleCat.free k).obj PUnit)`
170	   packaged as an iso.
171	3. `(Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique k k PUnit).toModuleIso` —
172	   `(ModuleCat.free k).obj PUnit ≅ ModuleCat.of k k`. -/
173	noncomputable def HModule'_top_sourceIso
174	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
175	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
176	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
177	    {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
178	    (presheafToSheaf J _).obj
179	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj T)
180	      ≅ (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) :=
181	  (presheafToSheaf J _).mapIso
182	    (Functor.isoWhiskerRight
183	        (NatIso.ofComponents
184	          (fun _ => Equiv.toIso { toFun := fun _ => PUnit.unit
185	                                  invFun := fun _ => hT.from _
186	                                  left_inv := fun _ => hT.hom_ext _ _
187	                                  right_inv := fun _ => rfl })
188	          (fun _ => by ext; rfl))
189	        (ModuleCat.free k) ≪≫
190	      Functor.constComp _ PUnit.{u+1} (ModuleCat.free k) ≪≫
191	      (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).mapIso
192	        (Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique k k PUnit).toModuleIso)
193	
194	/-- Iter-032 cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u+1`. Combining the
195	iter-031 `HModule'_top_sourceIso` (a sheaf-level natural iso between source
196	objects of two `Ext` computations) with the Mathlib Ext-API operation
197	`precompOfLinear` (pre-composition of `Ext` along an `Ext 0`-element) yields
198	a `k`-linear equivalence between the universe-`u+1` Ext at the sheafified
199	representable on `T` and `HModule k F n`.
200	
201	The universe annotation `Abelian.Ext.{u+1}` on the LHS is load-bearing —
202	without it Lean would pick `Ext.{u}` (matching iter-014's `HModule'` choice),
203	and the equivalence would not typecheck against `HModule k F n : Type (u+1)`
204	(iter-009). By pinning the LHS to `Ext.{u+1}` we sidestep the iter-031
205	universe mismatch (`HModule' : Type u` vs `HModule : Type (u+1)`); both
206	sides of this equivalence live at `Type (u+1)`. The bridge from
207	`HModule' k F n T : Type u` to the LHS here is iter-033+ work — most
208	plausibly via a small refactor on iter-014 to align `HModule'`'s universe,
209	or via a `ULift.{u+1}` wrapper.
210	
211	Body uses `LinearEquiv.ofLinear` with two pre-composition maps via the
212	iter-031 iso's hom/inv. The round-trip equations close by a four-step
213	rewrite: associativity (with the middle factor degree 0), composition
214	collapse via `mk₀_comp_mk₀`, the iso identity, and `mk₀_id_comp`. -/
215	noncomputable def HModule_top_linearEquiv
216	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
217	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
218	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
219	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
220	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
221	    Abelian.Ext.{u+1}
222	        ((presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
223	          ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj T)) F n
224	      ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n :=
225	  let α := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT
226	  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
227	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
228	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
229	    (by
230	      ext x
231	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp
232	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp x (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = x
233	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
234	          α.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
235	    (by
236	      ext y
237	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp
238	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp y (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = y
239	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
240	          α.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
241	
242	/-- Iter-033 cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u`. The universe-`u`
243	parallel of iter-032's `HModule_top_linearEquiv`. Combining the iter-031
244	`HModule'_top_sourceIso` (a sheaf-level natural iso between source objects
245	of two `Ext` computations) with the Mathlib Ext-API operation
246	`precompOfLinear` (pre-composition of `Ext` along an `Ext 0`-element)
247	yields a `k`-linear equivalence between `HModule' k F n T` (the iter-014
248	universe-`u` cohomology of an open at the terminal) and the universe-`u`
249	`Ext` at the constant sheaf on `ModuleCat.of k k`.
250	
251	Both sides live at `Type u` (matching iter-014's `HModule'` ascription).
252	This is the universe-`u` parallel of iter-032's universe-`u+1` form; the
253	final bridge between the two (`Ext.{u}((constantSheaf J _).obj
254	(ModuleCat.of k k)) F n ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n = Ext.{u+1}(...)`) is
255	iter-034+ work, requiring Mathlib's `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` (a bare
256	`Equiv`, file `Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean`
257	L540) to be upgraded to a `LinearEquiv`.
258	
259	The body is shape-identical to iter-032: `LinearEquiv.ofLinear` with two
260	pre-composition maps via the iter-031 iso's hom/inv. The round-trip
261	equations close by the same four-step rewrite chain. Lean's universe
262	inference adapts automatically because the LHS `HModule' k F n T`
263	forces `Ext.{u}` everywhere. -/
264	noncomputable def HModule'_top_linearEquiv
265	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
266	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
267	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
268	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
269	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
270	    HModule' k F n T
271	      ≃ₗ[k] Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
272	              (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n :=
273	  let α := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT
274	  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
275	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
276	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
277	    (by
278	      ext x
279	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp
280	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp x (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = x
281	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
282	          α.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
283	    (by
284	      ext y
285	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp
286	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp y (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = y
287	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
288	          α.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])

290	/-- Iter-034 universe-bump bridge. Composes iter-033's
291	`HModule'_top_linearEquiv` (universe-`u` cover-totality between `HModule' k F n T`
292	and `Ext.{u} ((constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n`) with the
293	universe shift `Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` (Mathlib gap-fill at the top
294	of this file) lifting the result to `Ext.{u+1} ((constantSheaf J _).obj

301	    (k : Type u) [Field k]

303	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
304	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
305	    [HasExt.{u + 1} (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
306	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
307	    HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n :=
308	  (HModule'_top_linearEquiv k F n hT).trans Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv

33	/-! ## Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill: `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` as a `LinearEquiv`
34	
35	Mathlib's `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ Ext.{w'} X Y n`
36	(`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540) is a bare
37	`Equiv` only. The iter-034 universe-bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv`
38	(below in `section CoverTotality`) requires it as a `LinearEquiv` over the
39	`Linear R C`-enriched abelian category. The upgrade is mechanical: the
40	`AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` and `Module R (Ext X Y n)` instances are themselves
41	transferred from the standard derived category through `homEquiv`, and
42	Mathlib's load-bearing intermediate lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv` (Basic.lean
43	L543–545) shows that `chgUniv` preserves the underlying derived-category
44	morphism. So `chgUniv` automatically commutes with `+` and `•`. Two short
45	helper lemmas (`chgUniv_add` and `chgUniv_smul`) plus a 7-line `LinearEquiv`
46	structure literal give the upgrade.
47	
48	This cohort lives outside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` because the
49	helpers belong to the `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext` namespace. We use the
50	qualified-name pattern `Abelian.Ext.foo` rather than wrapping the entire file
51	in a second namespace block. -/
52	
53	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (a): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is additive. The
54	underlying `homEquiv` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean`
55	L162) is preserved by `chgUniv` (Mathlib lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv`,
56	L543–545), and the `AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` instance (L241–243) is defined by
57	transferring the `AddCommGroup` from the standard derived category through
58	`homEquiv`. Combining these via the `Ext.ext` extensionality (L177–179) gives
59	additivity. -/
60	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_add
61	    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
62	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ℕ} (a b : Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n) :
63	    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b) =
64	      Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a + Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b := by
65	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
66	  ext
67	  rw [Abelian.Ext.add_hom, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply,
68	      ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply]
69	  change Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b)) =
70	    Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a) +
71	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b)
72	  rw [Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv,
73	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, ← Abelian.Ext.add_hom]
74	
75	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (b): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is `R`-linear when `C`
76	is `Linear R`-enriched. Same proof shape as `chgUniv_add`, but using
77	`smul_hom` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Linear.lean` L52–55)
78	in place of `add_hom`. -/
79	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_smul
80	    {R : Type*} [Ring R] {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [Linear R C]
81	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ℕ}
82	    (r : R) (a : Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n) :
83	    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (r • a) = r • Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a := by
84	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
85	  ext
86	  rw [Abelian.Ext.smul_hom, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply,
87	      ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply]
88	  change Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (r • a)) =
89	    r • Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a)
90	  rw [Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv,
91	      ← Abelian.Ext.smul_hom]
92	
93	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (c): the `R`-linear upgrade of Mathlib's bare-`Equiv`
94	`Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ Ext.{w'} X Y n` to a `LinearEquiv`,
95	when `C` is `Linear R`-enriched abelian. Combines (a) and (b). The
96	`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540 declaration is
97	universe-polymorphic but only proves the underlying bijection; this wrapper
98	adds the algebraic structure preservation needed for downstream `LinearEquiv`
99	chaining (specifically, the iter-034 universe bridge
100	`HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` below). -/
101	noncomputable def Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv
102	    {R : Type*} [Ring R] {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [Linear R C]
103	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ℕ} :
104	    Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ₗ[R] Abelian.Ext.{w'} X Y n where
105	  toFun a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a
106	  invFun a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w} a
107	  left_inv a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'}.left_inv a
108	  right_inv a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'}.right_inv a
109	  map_add' a b := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_add a b
110	  map_smul' r a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_smul r a

114	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the substantive functor mirroring
115	Mathlib's `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheafFunctor` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Sends
116	a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` to the presheaf
117	`X ↦ Ext^n((presheafToSheaf J _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X), F)`,
118	i.e.\ to a presheaf `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is
119	`HModule' k F n X` (definitionally, see `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` below).
120	
121	The codomain is `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` (not `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat k`) because Mathlib's
122	`Abelian.extFunctor n : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ C ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` always lands in `AddCommGrpCat`
123	regardless of the source category's `Linear`-enrichment; the per-fiber `Module k`
124	structure on `(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op X)` is preserved
125	through the iter-014 `HModule'` reducibility chain. Probe-confirmed body
126	(iter-016 plan-agent). Used downstream as the prerequisite for the queued
127	iter-017+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris LES. -/
128	noncomputable def HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor
129	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
130	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
131	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
132	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
133	    (n : ℕ) :
134	    Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k) ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
135	  Functor.flip
136	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
137	      presheafToSheaf _ _).op ⋙ Abelian.extFunctor n)
138	
139	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the abbrev wrapper mirroring Mathlib's
140	`Sheaf.cohomologyPresheaf` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Evaluates the
141	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` at a sheaf `F`, giving a presheaf
142	`Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is `HModule' k F n X` definitionally
143	(probe-confirmed `rfl` by the iter-016 plan-agent). The `noncomputable abbrev`
144	form is required for the per-fiber definitional identification with `HModule'`;
145	under `def` the wrapper would block the `rfl`-level reduction that downstream
146	Mayer-Vietoris and Stein-factorization arguments rely on. -/
147	noncomputable abbrev HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf
148	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
149	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
150	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
151	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
152	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
153	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
154	  (HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor k J n).obj F
155	
156	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the first Mayer-Vietoris LES building
157	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the sum of the two restriction maps
158	`(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₂₄.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₃₄.op`,
159	as a single map into the biproduct. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
160	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod` (file
161	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L43–46) for
162	the `ModuleCat k` flavor with `F.cohomologyPresheaf → HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F`.
163	
164	The codomain `(... ).obj (op S.X₂) ⊞ (...).obj (op S.X₃)` is the biproduct in
165	`AddCommGrpCat` (Mathlib `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Biproducts.lean`); since
166	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n` is `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`, the biproduct
167	auto-resolves. Probe-confirmed body (iter-017 plan-agent). Used downstream as the
168	first of three Mayer-Vietoris LES building blocks; iter-018 will add the
169	composition-is-zero lemma and the connecting hom `δ`, iter-019 the LES sequence
170	and exactness theorem. -/
171	noncomputable def HModule'_toBiprod
172	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
173	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
174	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
175	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
176	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
177	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) ⟶
178	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
179	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) :=
180	  biprod.lift ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op)
181	              ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op)
182	
183	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the second Mayer-Vietoris LES building
184	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the difference of the two restriction maps
185	`(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₂.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₃.op`,
186	as a single map out of the biproduct. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
187	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.fromBiprod` (file
188	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L67–70) for
189	the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
190	
191	The negation `-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op` uses the
192	preadditive structure on `AddCommGrpCat`-morphisms; the sign is the standard
193	Mayer-Vietoris convention encoding the alternating-sum structure of the Čech
194	complex. Probe-confirmed body (iter-017 plan-agent). -/
195	noncomputable def HModule'_fromBiprod
196	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
197	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
198	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
199	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
200	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
201	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
202	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) ⟶
203	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) :=
204	  biprod.desc ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₂.op)
205	              (-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op)
206	
207	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-018): the third Mayer-Vietoris LES building
208	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the composition-is-zero lemma asserting
209	`HModule'_toBiprod k S F n ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n = 0`. Direct mirror
210	of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod_fromBiprod`
211	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`,
212	L72–75) for the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
213	
214	The proof unwinds via the biproduct universal property
215	(`biprod.lift_desc : biprod.lift a b ≫ biprod.desc c d = a ≫ c + b ≫ d`),
216	the preadditive negation (`Preadditive.comp_neg`), the addition-of-negation
217	arithmetic (`← sub_eq_add_neg`), the zero-iff-equal arithmetic
218	(`sub_eq_zero`), the contravariant functoriality of `cohomologyPresheaf`
219	(`← Functor.map_comp`, `← op_comp`), and the Mayer-Vietoris square
220	factorisation `S.toSquare.fac` (which says `S.f₁₂ ≫ S.f₂₄ = S.f₁₃ ≫ S.f₃₄`).
221	Probe-confirmed proof (iter-018 plan-agent); the `simp only` set transfers
222	verbatim from Mathlib's `AddCommGrpCat`-flavored proof because every step
223	is value-category-agnostic.
224	
225	The `@[reassoc (attr := simp)]` attribute generates the post-composition
226	form `… ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod … ≫ Z = 0 ≫ Z = 0` and registers the lemma
227	as a `simp` lemma; both are required for downstream Mayer-Vietoris LES
228	exactness arguments. -/
229	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
230	lemma HModule'_toBiprod_fromBiprod
231	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
232	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
233	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
234	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
235	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
236	    HModule'_toBiprod k S F n ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n = 0 := by
237	  simp only [HModule'_toBiprod, HModule'_fromBiprod, biprod.lift_desc,
238	    Preadditive.comp_neg, ← sub_eq_add_neg, sub_eq_zero,
239	    ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, S.toSquare.fac]
240	
241	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019 helper, Mathlib gap-fill): `ModuleCat.free k`
242	is left adjoint to `forget (ModuleCat k)`. Mathlib's
243	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean` provides the adjunction
244	`ModuleCat.adj k : ModuleCat.free k ⊣ forget (ModuleCat k)` but does not register
245	the corresponding `IsLeftAdjoint` instance (whereas `AddCommGrpCat.free.IsLeftAdjoint`
246	is registered in `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Adjunctions.lean` L84). This
247	project-local instance fills the gap so that `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k)`
248	acquires the necessary `PreservesColimit` instance, used by
249	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf` below. -/
250	instance ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint
251	    (k : Type u) [Field k] : (ModuleCat.free k).IsLeftAdjoint :=
252	  ⟨_, ⟨ModuleCat.adj k⟩⟩
253	
254	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
255	pushout-of-free-sheaves analog of Mathlib's
256	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.isPushoutAddCommGrpFreeSheaf`
257	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean`, L154–160).
258	States that the image of the Mayer-Vietoris square `S` under the composite
259	`yoneda ⋙ Functor.whiskeringRight ⋅ (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙ presheafToSheaf J _`
260	is a pushout square in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. The proof transfers the
261	type-level pushout `S.isPushout` through `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k)`
262	(which preserves pushouts because `(ModuleCat.free k).IsLeftAdjoint`, registered
263	in `ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint` above) and adjusts via the canonical iso
264	`presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso`.
265	
266	Probe-confirmed body (iter-019 plan-agent); the proof is verbatim from Mathlib L156–160
267	with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. Used downstream in iter-020+ to
268	prove `Mono` / `Epi` / `Exact` instances on the iter-019 short complex
269	`HModule'_shortComplex`, and ultimately in the iter-022+ `δ` connecting hom. -/
270	lemma HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf
271	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
272	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
273	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
274	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
275	    (S.map (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
276	      presheafToSheaf J _)).IsPushout :=
277	  (S.isPushout.map (Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k))).of_iso
278	    ((Square.mapFunctor.mapIso
279	      (presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso J (ModuleCat.free k))).app
280	        (S.map yoneda))

282	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
283	short complex of free sheaves underlying the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
284	Direct mirror of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.shortComplex`
285	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean`, L234–249) for the
286	`ModuleCat k` flavor with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
287	
288	The objects are the sheafified free `ModuleCat k`-valued presheaves on the four
289	vertices of the Mayer-Vietoris square (with the middle pair `S.X₂` and `S.X₃`
290	biproduct-summed). The two morphisms are: `f` (the difference `(yoneda.map S.f₁₂)
291	– (yoneda.map S.f₁₃)` lifted through the biproduct), and `g` (the sum
292	`(yoneda.map S.f₂₄) + (yoneda.map S.f₃₄)` desced through the biproduct).
293	The `zero` proof (i.e.\ `f ≫ g = 0`) follows from the
294	`cokernelCofork.condition` of the pushout square in
295	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf` above.
296	
297	Probe-confirmed body (iter-019 plan-agent); structure-literal mirror of Mathlib L235–249
298	with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The `@[simps]` attribute generates
299	field-projection simp lemmas (`HModule'_shortComplex_X₁`, `..._X₂`, `..._X₃`,
300	`..._f`, `..._g`) consumed by the iter-020+ `Mono`/`Epi`/`Exact`/`ShortExact`
301	proofs and the iter-022+ `δ` definition. -/
302	@[simps]
303	noncomputable def HModule'_shortComplex
304	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
305	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
306	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
307	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
308	    ShortComplex (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) where
309	  X₁ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₁ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
310	  X₂ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₂ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k) ⊞
311	    (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₃ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
312	  X₃ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₄ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
313	  f :=
314	    biprod.lift
315	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₁₂) _))
316	      (-(presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₁₃) _))
317	  g :=
318	    biprod.desc
319	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₂₄) _))
320	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₃₄) _))
321	  zero :=
322	    (S.map (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
323	        presheafToSheaf J _)).cokernelCofork.condition

325	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020 helper, Mathlib gap-fill): the free-module
326	functor `ModuleCat.free k : Type u ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k` preserves monomorphisms.
327	Mathlib registers `AddCommGrpCat.instPreservesMonomorphismsFree :
328	AddCommGrpCat.free.PreservesMonomorphisms` in
329	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Adjunctions.lean` but does not register the
330	corresponding instance for `ModuleCat.free k` in
331	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean`. This project-local
332	instance fills the gap so that, after `simp only [biprod.lift_snd]`, the
333	typeclass-search engine can discharge the residual `Mono` goal in the proof
334	of `HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono` below.
335	
336	The proof: for an injective function `f : X → Y` between types,
